title: memoryreplay

funding_references.funder_name: R.D. is supported by Wellcome Trust Investigator Award 098362/ Z/12/Z.
Y.L. is supported by a UCL Graduate Research Scholarship and an Overseas Research Scholarship.
The Max Planck University College London Centre is a joint initiative supported by University College London and the Max Planck Society.

description: This MEG dataset contains neural recordings from a memory replay study investigating how the brain replays sequential memories during rest and task periods. Participants performed a memory encoding phase followed by a localizer phase while MEG activity was recorded. The dataset includes detailed trigger information for event marking, stimulus category labels, and story-ending classifications, enabling analysis of memory sequence representations in neural activity.
methods_description: MEG recordings were acquired during two experimental phases: a memory phase where participants viewed images from 6 stimulus categories plus story-ending stimuli, and a localizer phase with similar stimulus presentations. Event onsets were marked by trigger pulses in the MEG data, with category information encoded in trigger values (1-6 for stimulus categories, 7-8 for positive/negative story endings). Visual stimulus onset delays of approximately 20 ms relative to triggers were estimated using photodiode measurements. Baseline periods were defined as 2 seconds prior to picture onset during the localizer phase.
